channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: The right’s embrace of Adam Carolla cost him friends and gigs — but not his edge
item.description: The podcaster says he isn’t interested in climbing the late-night ladder or striking big deals as much as being able to say — often vulgarly — whatever he wants.

item.title: Trump’s first surgeon general tries to stop nominee from becoming his second
item.description: Jerome Adams, who served at the height of the coronavirus pandemic, says Casey Means is unqualified to hold the job. Means’s nomination is stalled in the Senate.

item.title: Before an elevator door severed her arm, tenants complained about safety
item.description: Tijuanna Fisher lost her arm after an elevator door closed on her dog’s leash while Fisher was still in the elevator. Video obtained by The Washington Post shows how quickly the incident occurred in the historic D.C. building, once home to a young JFK.

item.title: Iranian missile infrastructure is strained after weeks of U.S.-Israeli strikes
item.description: Satellite imagery analysis reveals the extent of the destruction at Iranian ballistic missile production and launch sites, undermining Iran’s central military strategy. But some experts said they doubted the missile program could ever be completely destroyed.

item.title: ICE agents may remain at airports even after TSA agents get paid this week
item.description: As the partial government shutdown drags on, border czar Tom Homan said Sunday that some of the decision about whether to keep immigration agents at airports will depend on whether TSA agents “come back to work.”

item.title: Still need to finish this weekend’s On the Record? We’ve got you.
item.description: Play now to finish out your score for the week.

item.title: This man has rarely voted and embraces his role in Jan. 6. Now he’s running for office.
item.description: Adam Johnson became infamous for taking House Speaker Nancy Pelosi’s lectern during the riot at the Capitol. Now, Floridians will decide whether a man who tried to upend democracy can help lead one.

item.title: Crossword and a coffee: Solve the Sunday puzzle now
item.description: Crossword and a coffee: Solve the Sunday puzzle now

item.title: Invoking faith in wartime, Pete Hegseth breaks norms and worries critics
item.description: The defense secretary is upending decades-old norms, current and former leaders say, with some cautioning that his proselytizing violates the Constitution and undermines troop cohesion.

item.title: Pentagon prepares for weeks of ground operations in Iran, officials say, as thousands of U.S. troops arrive in Middle East
item.description: If President Donald Trump approves the plans, such an effort would mark a new phase of the war that could be significantly more dangerous to U.S. personnel than the first four weeks. Any potential ground operation would fall short of a full-scale invasion and could instead involve raids by a mixture of Special Operations forces and conventional infantry troops, said the officials.

item.title: The Philly cheesesteak is his family legacy. He dared to change the recipe.
item.description: Frankie Olivieri’s decision to tinker with the classic cheesesteak was driven by a decades-long need to break free of his father’s rigid ways.

item.title: MAGA’s eruption has so far kept Trump from endorsing in key Senate race
item.description: President Donald Trump was prepared to back Sen. John Cornyn in Texas. After supporters of Ken Paxton protested, Trump — so far — has backed off.

item.title: Kash Patel’s push against Democratic lawmaker raises concerns within FBI
item.description: The FBI director is seeking to post documents on Rep. Eric Swalwell (California) despite no public evidence of wrongdoing, officials said.

item.title: Why millions of seniors have suddenly lost health care coverage
item.description: The privatized version of Medicare — Medicare Advantage — grew rapidly with perks like vision and dental care. In some regions options are dwindling.

item.title: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.
item.description: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.

item.title: Iran-backed Houthis in Yemen launch attack on Israel for first time in war, an escalation in conflict engulfing Middle East
item.description: The missile attack by the Houthis in Yemen raises the prospect of further disruption to shipping, which has already seen energy prices spike and global markets roiled after Iran’s effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: DHS shutdown drags on after House GOP rejects Senate bill, passes its own
item.description: Senate Democrats have said the plan to fund the Department of Homeland Security, which has gone unfunded since Feb. 14, is “dead on arrival” in the upper chamber.

item.title: At least 10 U.S. troops wounded, two seriously, in Iranian attack on Saudi air base, officials say
item.description: The strike on Prince Sultan Air Base also damaged at least two Air Force refueling aircraft and exposed that despite weeks of strikes, Iran still poses a missile threat.

item.title: 13 surprising ways GLP-1s may benefit the body, according to science
item.description: From FDA-approved uses to emerging research, we break down what’s proven, promising and still theoretical.

item.title: Financial markets hit new lows for 2026 amid rising oil prices, consumer gloom
item.description: Investor concerns were driven by energy prices, with gas prices at the pump shooting up to a national average of $3.98, a dollar higher than a month ago. And prices are expected to worsen in coming days, with Brent crude oil prices above $113 a barrel Friday, up 50 percent from last month.

item.title: TSA agents will get paychecks as soon as Monday, DHS says, after Trump signs executive order to pay workers amid congressional stalemate
item.description: House Republicans plan to offer an eight-week measure to fund the Department of Homeland Security after rejecting a proposal that passed in the Senate.

item.title: House GOP rejects DHS funding bill passed by Senate, considers alternative
item.description: The inter-chamber squabble appears likely to lead to a continued stalemate over the department that has gone unfunded since Feb. 14, leading to long security lines at some airports.

item.title: Carolyn Hax chat: My sister wants to have brunch with our abusive parents. I’m not sure I can.
item.description: Advice columnist Carolyn Hax answers your questions about the strange train we call life.

item.title: DHS funding bill appears in danger in House after Senate passage
item.description: House Republicans have raised concerns that the deal does not include the Save America Act — a sweeping voting bill championed by President Donald Trump that House Republicans have demanded the Senate pass — and that it would not appropriate money for the administration’s deportation efforts.

item.title: Trump’s bluff in Iran is a ‘disaster’ of his own
item.description: Iran is exposing the limits of a presidency built on bluff, improvisation and submission rituals, Fareed Zakaria writes.

item.title: U.S. uses hundreds of Tomahawk missiles on Iran, alarming some at Pentagon
item.description: More than 850 Tomahawks have been fired in just four weeks, people familiar with the matter said, alarming some Pentagon officials because the weapon’s supply is limited.

item.title: House Democrat violated ethics rules, panel finds, putting her seat in jeopardy
item.description: The Ethics Committee panel cited a years-long inquiry into whether Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick misused covid pandemic money. She has maintained her innocence.

item.title: 10 questions, and don't forget the bonus wager! Finish off your week with On the Record.
item.description: Think you’ve got what it takes to earn a perfect score? Take today’s longer news quiz and complete your point total for the week.

item.title: Issue of pay for TSA officers now rests with the House after the Senate voted early this morning to fund much of DHS
item.description: The Senate bill would fund Transportation Security Administration and the rest of Department of Homeland Security except for Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Border Patrol.

item.title: Iran dilemma. Shutdown vote. Anthropic ruling. Kennedy Center. GLP-1 benefits. Summer destinations. Clever pig. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Judge blocks Pentagon order branding Anthropic a national security risk, calling its underlying logic ‘Orwellian’
item.description: The federal judge said officials had likely violated the law and retaliated against the firm for speaking publicly about how it wanted its technology to be used. Anthropic wanted a bar on its technology being used in mass domestic surveillance and to power fully autonomous weapons.

item.title: Trump to sign executive order calling for TSA officers to be paid amid budget impasse
item.description: The president posted online that his order would instruct Markwayne Mullin, the secretary of Homeland Security, to immediately pay the agents. The move would sidestep Congress as millions of travelers face record security delays, largely because of staffing problems at the Transportation Security Administration.

item.title: Education Department to abandon D.C. headquarters building, hand it to Department of Energy
item.description: It is the Trump administration’s latest and most visible move toward eliminating the Education Department altogether.

item.title: Trump says U.S. to hold off for 10 days on striking Iran’s energy sites
item.description: Trump issued a 48-hour ultimatum to Iran last weekend, saying he would “obliterate” the country’s power plants, beginning with the largest, if it did not reopen the Strait of Hormuz. He issued a five-day timeline on Monday, saying negotiations to end the war had begun.

item.title: White House weighing plan to pay TSA officers if Senate cannot quickly agree to a deal on Department of Homeland Security’s budget
item.description: The move would sidestep Congress as millions of travelers face record security delays, largely because of staffing problems at the Transportation Security Administration.

item.title: White House applauds ban on transgender competitors in women’s Olympics events
item.description: The International Olympic Committee announced that only biologically female athletes can compete in women’s sports at the Los Angeles Games in 2028.

item.title: Kennedy Center begins layoffs, rocking institution ahead of two-year closure
item.description: According to several staff members, the people cut include Nick Meade and Rick Loughery, whom Richard Grenell installed in top roles after Trump’s takeover of the center.

item.title: Far fewer immigrants are moving to big cities in U.S., data shows
item.description: New York, Los Angeles and Chicago are among the metro areas seeing steep declines in net immigration amid the Trump administration’s crackdown.

item.title: Savannah Guthrie gives emotional first interview since mom’s disappearance
item.description: Speaking with former “Today” co-host Hoda Kotb, Guthrie described the days following the alleged abduction of her 84-year-old mother, Nancy.

item.title: Bill Maher is Kennedy Center’s Twain Prize recipient, despite previous White House denial
item.description: Maher, the comedian whose relationship with President Donald Trump has veered between friendly and antagonistic, will be the 27th recipient of the Mark Twain Prize for American Humor and the first Twain Prize recipient chosen under Trump’s leadership — despite the fact that, just last week, the White House flatly denied this to be true.

item.title: Graham Platner’s past can’t overshadow the working man’s blues
item.description: The controversial Democrat is still piquing Mainers’ interest by channeling their frustrations.

item.title: An experimental vaccine in today’s On the Record
item.description: Answer now to earn your Thursday points.

item.title: Pentagon considers diverting Ukraine military aid to the Middle East
item.description: Although a final decision to redirect the equipment has not yet been made, the shift would highlight the growing trade-offs required to sustain the U.S. war against Iran.

item.title: D.C. cherry blossoms burst into peak bloom, earlier than normal yet again
item.description: Propelled by March temperatures running about 5 degrees above normal, the March 26 bloom date ranks among the top 20 earliest in more than a century of records.

item.title: Iran plan. Meta verdict. Airport lines. ‘Groyper’ anxiety. Loan shock. AI hate. Gut expert’s meal plan. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: This simple shift can help you thrive later in life, study shows
item.description: Researchers found that despite stereotypes, nearly half of adults over 65 did better on tests of cognitive and physical function as they aged.

item.title: Billy Ray and Miley Cyrus bury the hatchet on Disney’s ‘Hannah Montana’ special
item.description: In 2011, just after the final episode aired on Disney, Billy Ray told GQ that the show “destroyed” his family. But he and his daughter have since mended their relationship, leading to his appearance on the 20th anniversary show.

item.title: Iraq accuses U.S. of killing 7 soldiers in strike on military base’s clinic
item.description: As Iraq claimed “heinous aggression,” the U.S. denied it targeted a clinic. The incident threatens to sour relations between the countries amid the Iran war.

item.title: House votes to extend to 2029 Trump’s task force that ramped up policing, immigration enforcement in D.C.
item.description: The legislation, which must still pass the Senate, seeks to codify an executive order signed by President Donald Trump that, among other things, led to ramped-up immigration enforcement in the city.

item.title: White House delays nominating new CDC director ahead of today’s deadline
item.description: The responsibilities of leading the agency will remain with Jay Bhattacharya, the head of the National Institutes of Health, who has been serving as acting CDC director since last month.
